sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 16.43 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.16)

statement.latex: Is there a partition of the group$_{ _1}({ Z}/3{ Z})$into three subsets whose complements do not contain cosets modulo infinite subgroups? (There is a partition into two such subsets.) { E.\,G.\,Zelenyuk} Yes, moreover, every infinite abelian group with finitely many involutions can be partitioned into infinitely many subsets such that every coset modulo an infinite subgroup meets each subset of t...
